adopters.name: **Type**: How you use Apicurio Registry (see types below)
**Description**: A brief description of your use case
**Since**: The year you started using Apicurio Registry
**Website**: A link to your organization's website (optional)
**End-user**
adopters.description: Organizations running Apicurio Registry in production environments

adopters.name: **Integration**
adopters.description: Organizations whose products integrate with Apicurio Registry

adopters.name: **Vendor**
adopters.description: Organizations that embed or distribute Apicurio Registry as part of a commercial product

adopters.name: Axual
adopters.description: Axual provides mission-critical streaming platforms based on open source Apache Kafka and open source Apicurio Registry

adopters.name: Castor
adopters.description: Castor uses Apicurio as its central schema registry, facilitating schema management and distribution for event streaming and inter-service communication purposes

adopters.name: IBM
adopters.description: Apicurio Registry is included as the schema registry component in IBM Event Streams

adopters.name: Libon
adopters.description: At Libon we use apicurio as our source of truth across all the organization for Kafka's AVRO schemas

adopters.name: Red Hat
adopters.description: Red Hat build of Apicurio Registry, part of Red Hat Application Foundations

adopters.name: ZenWave 360
adopters.description: ZenWave360 uses Apicurio Registry as schemas registry for storing and versioning canonical references for AsyncAPI + Avro
